title: How to run a sshd podman container? (my container gets a fatal: chroot("/usr/share/empty.sshd"): Operation not permitted [preauth])
body:
### Issue Description

I took an old Podman-Dockerfile, that was able to run under Fedora F34 without any problem. 
But unfortunately this container is running into a problem, when i try to call it from outside,
the container-sshd throws a:

server/container:   fatal: chroot("/usr/share/empty.sshd"): Operation not permitted `[preauth]`

while the client calls the server-contaner by:

`$  ssh  -CY @chasmash -p 9222 ./jlohn31/startjl.sh `

The idea is to run a Java-Desktop-App like a server-app, without the need to installl it on your local system. This configuration ran for 2 years without any problem, but something changed.

In order to enable the sshd i added a cap flag to the podman-exec:

`$ podman run -d -p 192.168.0.137:9222:22 --name app31_002 --cgroup-manager cgroupfs  -v ${DATA_STORE}/storage:/home/techuser/jlohn:z   -v ${DATA_STORE}/cRTE/varLogs:/var/log:z  --cap-add audit_write  jlohn31_002`

But the system is still rejecting the ssh. So my question is, what do i  have to do, to run a sshd on and inside Fedora installation?

------------------------------------
podman-4.4.1-1.fc37.x86_64




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman  build -t app31_002 -v ${DATA_STORE}/lb:/home/techuser/app:z   --cgroup-manager cgroupfs  .
2. podman run -d -p 192.168.0.137:9222:22 --name app31 --cgroup-manager cgroupfs  -v ${DATA_STORE}/lb:/home/techuser/app:z   -v ${DATA_STORE}/cRTE/varLogs:/var/log:z  --cap-add audit_write  app31_002
3.


### Describe the results you received

```
(client) $ ssh  -CY techuser@server -p 9222 ./app/startjl.sh
Connection closed by 192.168.0.137 port 9222

(container) $ systemctl status sshd
...
...
Feb 20 11:40:39 33a5a8d3f34f systemd[1]: Started sshd.service - OpenSSH server daemon.
Feb 20 11:40:42 33a5a8d3f34f sshd[155]: fatal: chroot("/usr/share/empty.sshd"): Operation not permitted [preauth]
Feb 20 12:29:32 33a5a8d3f34f sshd[220]: fatal: chroot("/usr/share/empty.sshd"): Operation not permitted [preauth]

```
A simple `ssh  techuser@server -p 9222 ` fails too with the same result.


### Describe the results you expected

I would like to see my app on my remote client.

comments.body: I guess you also need `--cap-add sys_chroot`
